steps.title: Identify the needed support
steps.content: The claim says some modern irrigation methods in the Southwest have a lineage that long predates Lake Marisol's completion in 1908. The quotation therefore must connect present-day irrigation methods or engineering principles to a much older source, not merely describe current irrigation or later growth.

steps.title: Reject later or vague evidence
steps.content: Choice A describes sprinkler systems developed in the late 20th century, after Lake Marisol. Choice B only says irrigation infrastructure matters today. Choice C describes irrigation growth after World War II, which is also much later than 1908.

steps.title: Use ancient continuity
steps.content: A 7th-century Hohokam system using hydraulic engineering design principles still used by today's engineers directly supports a long pre-1908 lineage for modern irrigation methods in the Southwest. The answer is D.
